label: Mid-2000s America
description: Post-9/11 era. SUVs and trucks at peak size. Flip phones everywhere, smartphones not yet. Big box retail dominant. Strip malls aging.
people_mix: a UPS driver wheeling packages on a hand truck
a group of college kids with messenger bags
a woman walking a small dog in a sweater
a man in cargo shorts talking on a flip phone
a teen with white earbuds and an MP3 player
a couple taking photos with a digital camera

specific_models: 2000-2007 Ford Taurus — rounded, aging fleet-look sedan
2003-2007 Honda Accord — clean lines, sharper than 1990s
2004-2008 Chrysler 300 — bold boxy retro chrome grille
2000-2006 Chevrolet Tahoe — full-size SUV peak era
2004-2008 Pontiac Grand Prix — sporty sedan plastic cladding
2001-2005 Honda Civic — rounded compact, very common
2005-2010 Jeep Grand Cherokee — rounded modern SUV
1998-2005 Volkswagen New Beetle — retro bubble shape
2001-2007 Dodge Grand Caravan — family minivan, rounded
2002-2008 Dodge Ram — big rig grille evolved

fuel.context: Rising prices, Hurricane Katrina spike in late 2005, SUV size at peak

characteristics: credit card readers at pump standard
LED price signs at gas stations
signage.typography_style: clean corporate sans-serif, polished brand identities

characteristics: early variable message signs on highways
green highway signs with Clearview or Highway Gothic font
LED traffic signals beginning to replace incandescent

items: parking meters individual and early multi-space

characteristics: cable and fiber lines on poles appearing
small satellite dishes on homes common

advertising.style: glossy digital photography, polished corporate brand imagery

lighting.street_lights: high pressure sodium — orange glow, early LED conversions rare
lighting.commercial: fluorescent standard, LED accent lighting beginning
lighting.signs: LED price signs, backlit plastic channel letters

photography.film_stock: early digital camera or Kodak/Fuji consumer film
photography.grain: low grain, early digital noise in shadows
photography.style: transitional film-to-digital, daylight snapshot, slightly soft focus

downtown_street.narrative: A 2005 main street — cellular stores and chain coffee arriving, the video store in its final days, flip phones in every hand.
storefronts: the video rental store closing down — 'EVERYTHING MUST GO — ALL MOVIES $3' banner across the window
the same diner with a refreshed awning and a 'COFFEE $1.25' vinyl decal on the glass
barber shop with its spinning barber pole, unchanged
cellular phone store with 'FREE PHONE WITH 2-YEAR PLAN' posters
espresso cafe with a chalkboard menu and a round-logo awning
video game store with a 'USED GAMES' sign and a midnight-release poster
tanning salon with an airbrushed sun mural on the window
bank branch modernized with internally lit channel letters
people_activities: a teenager texting on a flip phone with both thumbs
a woman in low-rise jeans talking on a flip phone
a man in cargo shorts and a trucker hat carrying shopping bags
two kids weaving down the sidewalk on razor scooters
a businessman with a Bluetooth earpiece gesturing as he walks
a shopper digging through the closing video store's sidewalk bargain bins
a barista handing an iced drink to a customer at the cafe door
a mother pushing a jogging stroller
a man loading a flat-screen TV box into an SUV
extras: the ghost-sign reduced to a faint trace of paint on the brick
a single scuffed payphone covered in stickers, rarely used
a bike rack with a couple of mountain bikes
a sandwich-board sign for the espresso cafe
a free real-estate flyer box on the corner
ribbon magnets on a lamppost
a sidewalk sandwich board with a cell-phone store promo

gas_station.narrative: A 2005 gas station — big multi-island canopy, card readers at every pump, LED price sign, and a convenience store doing most of the business.
storefronts: wide multi-island canopy with branded fascia lighting
tall pole sign with LED price digits
pumps with card readers and small pay-at-pump screens
large convenience store with energy-drink and phone-card ads in the glass
stacked cases of bottled water and charcoal bags outside the store
automated car wash tunnel beside the lot
coin-operated air machine near the back of the lot
people_activities: a driver self-serving at the pump
a woman inserting her card at the pump reader
a man talking on a flip phone while leaning against his SUV as it fuels
a clerk restocking the window display inside
a customer walking out with a coffee cup
a teenager filling tires at the coin-operated air machine
extras: 'NO SMOKING — TURN OFF ENGINE' decals on each pump
trash cans with paper-towel dispensers between the pumps
a propane exchange cage by the storefront
a free real-estate flyer box by the door
a windshield-washer fluid display rack by the pumps
ribbon magnets on the door of the service office

strip_mall.narrative: An aging 2005 strip mall — national franchises mixed with struggling locals, the video store on its last legs.
storefronts: video rental store with 'STORE CLOSING — EVERYTHING MUST GO' banners
cellular store with carrier logos and 'FREE FLIP PHONE' posters
sandwich franchise with backlit menu photos in the window
dollar store anchor with yellow 'EVERYTHING $1' signage
check-cashing store with a neon 'FAST CASH' sign
hair salon with a 'WALK-INS WELCOME' sign
video game store with a midnight-release poster in the window
tax preparation office with a green square logo
people_activities: a man loading shopping bags into an SUV's tailgate
teenagers gathered outside the game store
a woman texting on a flip phone under the canopy
a customer leaving the sandwich shop with a to-go bag
an employee dragging a clearance rack onto the walkway
a couple browsing the closing video store's bargain bins
extras: a 'STORE CLOSING' A-frame sign on the walkway
a cart corral in the lot
light poles with faded banner ads
a sidewalk sandwich board with a cell-phone store promo
a free real-estate flyer box by the walkway
ribbon magnets on a lamppost

default.narrative: An ordinary 2005 American commercial street — corporate channel-letter signs, flip phones, SUVs at the curb.
storefronts: local diner with a 'COFFEE $1.25' vinyl decal on the glass
barber shop with a spinning barber pole
video rental store with 'EVERYTHING MUST GO' closing banners
cellular phone store with contract posters
espresso cafe with a chalkboard menu
nail salon with an LED 'OPEN' sign
people_activities: a teenager texting on a flip phone
a man with a Bluetooth earpiece
a woman carrying an iced coffee
kids on razor scooters
a shopper with big-box store bags
a mother pushing a jogging stroller
extras: a faint trace of the old ghost-sign on the brick
a single scuffed payphone
a bike rack
a free real-estate flyer box
ribbon magnets on a lamppost
a sandwich board with a cell-phone store promo
